codeReview.summary: The partner-create-form feature has been implemented comprehensively. The implementation includes a complete form at /partners/new with all required partner fields, client-side and server-side validation, Indonesian language compliance, responsive design, and proper redirect after successful creation. The code follows TypeScript best practices with proper type definitions and uses the established project patterns for server functions with TanStack Start.

issues.description: Uses window.location.href for navigation instead of TanStack Router's router.navigate. While this works, it causes a full page reload instead of client-side navigation. Consider using the router instance from useRouter hook for better UX.
Same navigation pattern - uses window.location.href instead of router.navigate. Note the worker attempted to use router.navigate but faced issues, which is documented in the transcript.
Uses inline CSS-in-JS styles instead of Tailwind CSS classes which are used elsewhere in the project. This creates inconsistency in styling approach but doesn't affect functionality.
The setTimeout delay of 1500ms before redirecting after successful creation is a UX choice, but immediate redirect would also be acceptable. Current implementation is fine.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md specifies TanStack Router navigation conventions but doesn't explicitly require router.navigate over window.location.href. The worker faced difficulties with router.navigate (transcript shows edit failures) and fell back to window.location.href successfully. Consider documenting navigation patterns more explicitly in AGENTS.md.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows: 'Edit failed - router.navigate pattern not found'. Worker then used window.location.href which worked.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The fullstack-worker skill was followed correctly. Worker ran typecheck, manually verified with curl, and committed successfully. Skill feedback shows followedProcedure: true with only minor deviation for browser testing due to missing Playwright dependencies.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true. Deviation noted: 'Used curl for server verification due to missing Playwright dependencies'.

sharedStateObservations.observation: services.yaml correctly documents dev server on port 3002. Worker used correct port throughout testing.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: services.yaml shows 'port: 3002' for web service. Handoff shows verification commands using :3002.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker discovered and documented TanStack Router file-based routing limitation with dot notation (partners.new.tsx). Route tree auto-generation failed and manual edit was required. This knowledge should be captured in .factory/library/architecture.md or AGENTS.md for future workers.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.discoveredIssues[0]: 'The route tree auto-generation didn't pick up the new /partners/new route automatically - had to be manually added to routeTree.gen.ts. This is a known TanStack Router limitation.'

summary: The partner-create-form feature is fully implemented and meets all requirements. The form at /partners/new includes all partner fields with proper validation (required fields: full_name, partner_type; format validation: email, phone), uses Indonesian language throughout, implements responsive mobile-first design, and redirects to the partners list after successful creation. The server function createPartner properly validates input, handles database insertion via Prisma, and returns structured error messages. TypeScript types are comprehensive and typecheck passes. Minor non-blocking issues include using window.location.href for navigation instead of TanStack Router's navigate, and using inline styles instead of Tailwind. These don't impact functionality or user experience significantly. The feature fulfills VAL-PARTNER-004 (create new partner), VAL-PARTNER-005 (form validation), and VAL-UI-001 (Indonesian language).
